title: Bernardia interrupta
intro: Bernardia interrupta, also known as "Palo de Cristal" or "Glass Tree," is a shrub native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, as well as antimicrobial and antioxidant activities. The bioactive constituents of Bernardia interrupta include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Bernardia interrupta is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, making it a valuable resource for folk medicine in its native regions.
